reasoning: The threshold for resolving YES requires an extreme, 10-fold increase in the number of recorded protests within a 30-day window compared to the 30-day average over the preceding year. Based on the reference value, this would mean jumping from an average of ~85 protests per month to over 850 protests per month in China. Such an astronomical increase would either require a mass nationwide uprising (on the scale of the White Paper protests but much larger/more sustained) or a sudden and massive shift in ACLED's data collection methodology that introduces hundreds of new monthly events without retroactively updating the baseline. Currently, there are no ongoing mass protests crossing this threshold as of late June 2025. In the absence of a visible nationwide uprising or known methodological overhaul, the likelihood of this occurring in the very near term is essentially the minimum bound. Probabilities are kept very low but marginally increase for later horizons to reflect the fundamental uncertainty of potential black swan events (e.g., sudden severe regime instability or economic crisis) further out in 2025.
